title: PID 1, Signals, Shutdown, Health, Restart, and Resource Constraints · Docker Build Room
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working bounded process lifecycle and health policy for pid 1, signals, shutdown, health, restart, and resource constraints, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded process lifecycle and health policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.
Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery.
Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 640 MiB. Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits
steps.instruction:

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-cpu-memory-pids-limits-1h5c33
  - competency: docker-cpu-memory-pids-limits
    artifact: runtime-policy.yaml
    decision: cpu memory pids limits
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: native amd64 engine with a cold content store
    fault-injection: restore a volume under a different numeric user identity
    retained-proof: restored checksum and application-level consistency query
    case-key: 1h5c33
    change: |
      init: true
      stop_grace_period: 30s
      healthcheck: bounded-readiness-probe
      restart: on-failure:3
      memory: 256m
      pids_limit: 128
    verify: docker inspect training-worker --format json && docker events --since 10m
    failure: Reject an unbounded probe, restart loop as recovery, shell wrapper that swallows signals, or deployment without memory and PID limits.
    repair: Make PID 1 ownership explicit, bound shutdown and health timing, preserve durable work, and test termination plus resource pressure.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

Competency probe: Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Containers receive safe CPU and memory limits by default.
